summary: Comprehensive hover state testing completed for CSS fix. The fix successfully resolved the issue where dropdown items, buttons, and menu items were turning white/invisible on hover. All interactive elements now show proper hover states with visible text.

issues: Missing aria-describedby for DialogContent (accessibility warning in console)

passed_tests: User menu dropdown hover - Role and Logout items visible with proper highlight
Sidebar navigation hover - Items show darker navy blue with white text
Materials page filter dropdown hover - All category options visible
Add Material dialog Category dropdown hover - All 6 options visible with cream highlight
Add Material dialog UOM dropdown hover - All 7 options visible with cream highlight
Add Material dialog Stock Method dropdown hover - FIFO/LIFO visible with cream highlight
Dialog Cancel button hover - Proper outline variant hover state
Dialog Create Material button hover - Proper primary button hover state
GRN page table row hover - Subtle gray highlight visible
GRN Create dialog Material dropdown hover - All 10+ materials visible with cream highlight
Bins page table row hover - Subtle gray highlight visible
Bins page status filter dropdown hover - All 5 status options visible
Login page Sign In button hover - Amber button with white text visible
Login page Continue with Google button hover - Outline button with cream highlight visible
Login page Seed Demo Data link hover - Underline visible
Login page Register link hover - Amber text visible
critical_code_review_comments: CSS fix correctly renamed --accent to --wms-accent in first :root block to prevent collision with @layer base --accent
Changed @layer base --accent from bold amber (38 92% 50%) to subtle warm cream (38 40% 95%) for readable hover states
Changed --accent-foreground from white (0 0% 100%) to dark (220 14% 10%) ensuring text remains readable on hover
Primary buttons still use --primary (amber) for their background color - correct behavior maintained
context_for_next_testing_agent: CSS hover state fix verified working. The --accent CSS variable collision issue has been resolved. All dropdown items, ghost/outline buttons, and menu items now show proper hover states with visible dark text on subtle cream background. No further testing needed for this specific bug fix.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: CSS variable collision in index.css where --accent was defined as hex (#f59e0b) in unlayered :root, overriding the HSL value in @layer base. This caused hsl(#f59e0b) = invalid CSS, resulting in no background color on hover, making text appear white/invisible.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: 1) Renamed --accent to --wms-accent in first :root block. 2) Changed @layer base --accent to subtle warm cream (38 40% 95%) with dark foreground (220 14% 10%).
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All hover states across dropdowns, buttons, menus, and table rows now show proper visual feedback with readable text.
